observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to:
1. Strictly comply with the documentary requirements on the grant of
cash advances;
2. Avoid granting additional cash advances to any official or employee
unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper
accounting thereof is made; and
3. Issue demand letters, if necessary.
b. Special Disbursing officer to:
1. Comply with liquidation of cash advances within the reglementary
period; and
2. Submit the ROD to the Municipal Accountant within 10 days from the
close of the month.

We recommended and management agreed that Local Chief Executive and the
Local Legislative Chief Officers:
a. exercise due diligence in the approval of travel itineraries and to correct
claims which results to non-economical use of the meager resources of
government funds;
b. direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to charge
travelling and training expenses appropriately; and
c. direct the Municipal Accountant to issue letter of demand for unliquidated
cash advances.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
direct:
a. The Municipal Accountant to:
1. Prepare Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the properties below the
capitalization threshold and ensure that all future acquisitions of
properties below the capitalization threshold of ₱50,000.00 be recorded
in accordance with COA Circular No. 2022-004;
2. Take up necessary adjusting entries to recognize depreciation of the
abovementioned PPE accounts by corresponding prior years
adjustment in the books to be closed to the Government Equity Account;
and
3. Disclose in the notes to FS the useful lives of each category of recognized
PPE.
b. The Inventory Committee/Property Custodian to:
4. Formulate plans and strategize the complete conduct of physical count
of all its PPE, and prepared and submit the RPCPPE not later than
January 31 of the ensuing year in compliance with Section 124 of the
Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
5. Prepare and submit the IIRUP and other required documents in
accordance with the Manual on PPE Appraisal and Disposal to
facilitate the disposal of all unserviceable properties at the earliest
possible time, pursuant to Section 79 of PD No. 1445, Sections 379-381
of RA 7160, and in accordance with the procedures cited in COA Circular
No. 89-296.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Property
Custodian prepare and maintain the PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards,
respectively, in order to facilitate the periodic reconciliation if the PPE
accounts with results of the physical inventory taking.

We recommend and management agreed that the LCE require the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Make necessary reconciliations and adjustments to arrive at the correct
balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements; and
b. Verify the validity of the ₱5,712,441.69 Accounts Payable balance
carried over from CY 2020. Valid claims shall be paid, otherwise, cause
an adjustment for the unsupported claims.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and the:
a. Municipal Engineer to immediately submit justification on the
unimplemented and delayed projects; and
b. Bids and Awards Committee and Municipal Engineer to expedite the
procurement and implementation process to realize the programmed
projects as planned.

Moving forward, we recommend that the management optimally utilize the
20% DF and ensure that development projects are well-planned and are
procurement-and-implementation-ready.

We recommended and management agreed the Local Chief Executive to
require the Municipal Accountant to prepare correcting entry or adjustments
for the determined misstatements.

Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of Chapter 7, Vol 1 of Manual on
the NGAS for LGUs with regard to the proper Accounting entry in books for
Inventory Accounts.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned
personnel to immediately submit the pre and post-implementation
documentary requirements relative to the implementation of the TUPAD
program to verify the validity and propriety of payments.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Engineer to fully implement the projects upon receipt of funds in accordance
with the guidelines and stipulations set forth by the agency to prevent
reversion of funds and also for the benefit of the constituents/beneficiaries.

We recommended and management agreed that the LCE:
a. Direct the Municipal Assessor to provide the Assessment Roll to the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer;
b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant the
following: (a)Updated Real Property Tax Account Register and (b)Taxpayers
Index Card and Certified List of Taxpayers showing the name of taxpayers and
the amount due and collectible for the year; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to set up the appropriate amount of RPT and
SET Receivables for the year 2022 and appropriate amount of receivables for
the prior years.

We recommended that the management through the Municipal Treasurers
Office intensify the collection of the real property and special education taxes,
and avail of the remedies for the collection of the real property tax.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
direct :
a. The Municipal Accountant to compute accordingly the actual share of
barangays from the RPT collections accruing from CY 2021 to 2022; and
b. The Municipal Treasurer to immediately release the respective barangays
RPT share

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
submit the required certification and to direct the human resource personnel
to prepare individual contracts for each JO personnel which contain the
specific job description and the specific office to where he/she is assigned and
rates on a daily basis.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
direct the:
a. LDRRMO to expedite the implementation of PPAs under the yearly plan
to optimize the available disaster risk reduction fund intended for disaster
risk mitigation;
b. Municipal Budget Officer and LDRRMO to stop charging ineligible
expenses to the LDRRMF;
c. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to cause the complete
transfer of remaining unexpended balances from CY 2021-2022 LDRRMF
to the Trust Fund account;
d. Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary entries thereof and adjust the
balance of DRR Equipment account at year-end;
e. Municipal Accountant to properly disclose in the Notes to Financial
Statements the details of the balances of LDRRM Fund as shown in the
Annex D of COA Circular 2012-002 for fair presentation and to enhance
the understandability of the users of Financial Statements;

Moving forward, we likewise recommended that the Local Chief Executive
require the strict observance of the accounting and reporting guidelines for
the LDRRMF as enunciated under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated
September 12, 2012.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
(LCE) require the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to
submit the formulated policies/guidelines on the grant of AICS to the
Sanggunian for approval pursuant to Section 483(b) of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Collect and remit the amount of taxes under withheld for the above
transactions;
b. Regularly monitor compliance to withholding tax laws on all payments to
suppliers of goods and services by assigning a point person who will focus
on the appropriate withholding and timely remittance of withholding taxes.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to remit
withholding taxes on or before the 10th day of the following month from the
time the Expanded WT and WT on VAT/Percentage Taxes were withheld.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to remit
withholding taxes on or before the 10th day of the following month from the
time the Expanded WT and WT on VAT/Percentage Taxes were withheld.

1.12 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to (a) submit detailed schedule to
support the recording of adjustments; (b) prepare and submit the BRS
of all its depository bank accounts monthly, take up reconciling items,
and prepare necessary adjusting journal entries; (c) regularly reconcile
the book balances and bank records vis-a-vis the cashbook and prepare
adjusting entries, if any; (d) regularly submit the mandatory reports
and the supporting documents within the reglementary period; and (e)
draw a JEV to adjust the overstatement of the Cash in bank-LCCA
account by of ₱369,857.27 and reverse the journal entries made for
adjusting without supporting documents.
b) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to (a) post the balances of the Cash in
Bank- LCCA in the cashbook, with proper segregation of the sources
of funds; (b) coordinate with the depository bank to ensure the prompt
delivery of the monthly bank statements or its equivalent to facilitate
the timely reconciliation of the Cash in Bank accounts.

2.5 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Accountant
to (a) submit the lacking receipts to ascertain the accuracy of the balances; (b)
prepare and book the necessary adjustments; and (c) draw a JEV to adjust the
overstatement of the Cash – Local Treasury account.

3.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Enforce the immediate liquidation and/or refund of the unliquidated
cash advances and to refrain from granting additional cash advance to
officials and employees with outstanding balances until the previous
cash advances are fully settled /liquidated;
b. Designate a staff to closely monitor the liquidation of outstanding cash
advances;
c. Reconcile the unliquidated or outstanding cash advances made by the
Municipal Treasurer;
d. Perform the duties/responsibilities of the Accountant specified under
Item 7.0 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 and determine the unliquidated
cash advances that are non-moving for more than ten years where
settlement/collectability could no longer be ascertained and initiate the
request for write-off as allowed in the same Circular;
e. Send demand letters to all Officers and Employees as well as Non-LGU
personnel with unliquidated cash advances.

4.10 We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
a) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Voucher
to adjust the properties below the capitalization threshold and ensure
that all future acquisitions of properties below the capitalization
threshold of ₱15,000.00 will be recorded in accordance with COA
Circular 2020-006; and
b) Create an Inventory Committee, who are temporarily relieved of all
their regular duties, to complete the physical inventory in three (3)
months or less, and to prepare and submit the approved Physical
Inventory Plan to the COA Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar
days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities; and
ensure full implementation of “One-time cleansing of PPE
accounts” as provided for in COA Circular 2020-006.

5.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts under the three funds
and book the necessary adjustments to bring their balances into
agreement; and
b. Closely monitor transactions affecting reciprocal accounts and ensure
that these are recorded in the different funds simultaneously to avoid
any error or omission.

6.13 We recommended and the LCE assures the audit team that the programs,
projects and activities will be implemented during the budget year;

6.14 We likewise recommended and the LCE agreed to:
a) Require the Municipal Accountant to:
i. Transfer the unexpended balance of QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE
at the year-end be transferred to the STF Account; and the
subsequent recording of the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF account;
ii. Set up and maintain subsidiary ledgers for the STF for unutilized
DRRMF by year of transfer; receipts of National DRRM Fund
by transferring agency; DRRMF from other LGUs; and other
sources by the donor to readily determine the balances of
transfers/receipts; and
iii. Disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the
balances of Unexpended LDRRMF as shown in Annex D of COA
Circular No. 2012-002 for fair presentation and to enhance the
understandability of the users of Financial Statements.
b) Require the MDRRM Officer to prepare an annual LDRRMF Investment
Plan for the current and the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.

7.15 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require:
a. The concerned MHO personnel to submit immediately to the audit
team, thru the accountant, the following documents to establish their
claim to AHDP and SRAs under AO Nos. 35, 36 and 42:
i. Authority for the entitlement of the allowance/ office order/
authority to report for work;
ii. Daily Time Records with Biometric printouts/copies of
logbook or Certificate of Attendance in the COVID
Facility/Center by their immediate supervisor during the
period of claims;
iii. Accomplishment reports indicating the number of COVID
patients attended to in a day; and
iv. For health workers not directly catering COVID-19 patients,
an explanation as to the scientific basis for the premise that
healthcare workers not directly catering to COVID-19
patients are equally exposed to COVID-19 and can be
considered “in contact with COVID-19 patients”, and thus
should be given the AHDP and SRA.
b. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to Submit the
Liquidation Report under check#1421507, amounting to ₱341,002.00.

8.12 We recommend and the LCE agreed to:
a. Effect payment of goods and services through checks payable directly
to suppliers or dealers, and refrain from procuring goods and services
through reimbursement basis, except if the disbursements are petty
and may be charged to Petty Cash Fund;
b. Comply with the requirement of adequate procurement planning in
accordance with Section 7 of RA No. 9184; and
c. Consider the setting-up of Petty Cash Fund duly supported by an
estimate of expenses which shall be strictly for petty operating expenses
in accordance with` COA Circular No. 97-002.

9.5 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Accountant
and other officials concerned to immediately submit the disbursement
vouchers together with their supporting documents, and cancelled checks for
purposes of review and audit.

We recommended (1) that the Local Chief Executive demand immediately the turnover of undeposited collection amounting to P336,481.51 to the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and the latter to deposit the same to the LGU depository bank.
observations.reason: The previous Municipal Treasurer still failed to turnover the undeposited collection in spite of verbal demand by the LCE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to a) submit the bank statements for all bank accounts for the period September to December 2018; b) prepare a JEV for the cancellation of the stale checks; and c) see to it that the timely preparation of BRS for all bank accounts and to submit the same together with the bank statements to the Audit Team for review and verification.
observations.reason: Refer to Finding No. 1 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the specific public infrastructures acquired prior to CY 2015 to the appropriate Infrastructure Assets accounts and maintain property ledger cards for each of the public infrastructures. Likewise, prepare a lapsing schedule to compute depreciation of each depreciable PPE.
observations.reason: Refer to Finding No. 2 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from allocating portion of the 20% DF to non-programs/projects/activities which do not contribute to the attainment of the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU, contrary to Section 287 of RA 7160 and DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017. Moreover, a total of P1,122,850.00 was expended for items not chargeable to the fund while disbursements amounting to P3,276,439.92 lacked several supporting documents, contrary to Section 4(6) of PD 1445.
observations.reason: Refer to Finding No. 6 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) Executive Committee regularly monitor the implementation of the GPB to ensure that GAD PPAs are effectively and efficiently implemented as planned to attain the objectives of the GAD.
observations.reason: Refer to Finding No. 10 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers/Records for the balances of the controlling account appearing in the General Ledger and provide for the details of the non-moving/dormant accounts.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant recognize depreciation on property, plant and equipment for CY 2016 and 2015 in accordance with PPSAS No. 17 and direct the inventory committee to conduct physical count of PPE and render a report thereon, in accordance with Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and the Handbook on Property and Supply Management.
observations.reason: Physical count of PPE was still not conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant make correcting entries to effect the necessary adjustments to arrive at the correct balances of the unliquidated cash advances; prepare/maintain subsidiary ledgers for the balances of the advances accounts; and henceforth, exercise diligence in the posting of amounts to the general ledger.
observations.reason: No correcting entry was made to effect the adjustments in arriving at the correct balance of the cash advances; Subsidiary ledgers were still not maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management disburse funds only when supported with complete documentation as required under Section 4(6) of PD 1445. Likewise, require the Social Welfare Officer to formulate policies/guidelines on the grant of financial assistance and submit the same to sanggunian for approval pursuant to Section 483(b) of RA 7160.
observations.reason: The SWO still did not submit the guidelines on the grant of financial assistance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to render immediately and submit disbursement and liquidation vouchers together with supporting documents and to record all transactions pursuant to Section 347 of RA 7160 and COA Circulars.
observations.reason: Submission of disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents and verification were still delayed; hence, audit work was affected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to render their accounts and submit the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents immediately and to record all transactions in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160 and COA Circulars.
observations.reason: Some disbursement vouchers and supporting documents were still outstanding and inventory of missing items was yet to be completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive hire a Certified Public Accountant as head of the accounting unit, in compliance with Section 474 of RA 7160 and be directed to diligently perform all mandated functions of the Accountant.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the concerned
personnel to strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the
Local Road Asset Management System as provided in COA Circular No. 2015-
008.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to
compute the depreciation charges for the PPEs from CY 2015 to 2019 and
prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to update the carrying value of
these assets.

We recommended that the LCE directs the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal
Treasurer to prepare the required records and reports on the assessment of real
properties, as basis for booking-up of the RPT and SET receivables by the Municipal
Accountant. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to make correcting accounting
entries to establish the correct amount of the Deferred Real Property Tax Income and
Deferred Special Education Tax Income.

We recommended that Management submit the Memorandum of Agreement
for all fund transfers from National Government Agencies, and for fund transfer
from the DOLE, submit the documents on the screening and eligibility of
recipients and proof of receipt by the beneficiaries, together with the
registration documents.

Likewise, instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the proper
recording of transactions.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the guidelines on the
appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund and see to it that the
projects funded out of the fund which are included in the AIP are fully
implemented during the year in order that the intended constituents could
benefit out of these projects.

We recommended that management see to it that the LDRRMF be fully utilized
to support disaster risk reduction and management programs, projects and
activities, and instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal
Accountant to strictly observe the accounting and reporting guidelines set forth
in the above cited provisions.

We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Board actively perform
their mandated functions. Likewise, we recommended that only programs,
projects and activities related to the protection of public health and promotion of
solid waste reduction be included in the budget and fully implemented during the
year.

We recommended that the LCE require the HRMO to prepare individual
contracts for each JO personnel which contain the specific job description and
stating therein the work to be assigned to each personnel. Likewise, require the
JO personnel to prepare a detailed individual accomplishment reports stating the
quantity and actual work accomplished for a given period.

We further recommended that the LGU judiciously evaluate the necessity and
importance of the services of the JO personnel prior to hiring them, in order to
manage the scarce resources of the LGU.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) Executive
Committee regularly monitor the implementation of the GPB to ensure that GAD
PPAs are effectively and efficiently implemented as planned to attain the
objectives of the GAD.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require: 1. the Budget Officer to strictly appropriate the LDRRM fund for disaster prevention preparedness, disaster mitigation, response and recovery. Also, to maintain separate registries of appropriation, allotment and obligations for the unexpended LDRRMF of the previous years. 2. the MDRRM Officer to prepare an annual LDRRMF Investment Plan for the current and the previous years. 3. the Accountant to submit the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents totaling P2,723,007.00.
observations.managementAction: The MBO implemented the appropriation for the LDRRM fund for disaster prevention preparedness, response and mitigation. Also, to maintain separate registries, and the Accountant submitted the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents totaling P2,723,007.00.
observations.reason: The MBO implemented the appropriation for the LDRRM fund for disaster prevention preparedness, recovery. Also, the MBO prepared separate registries of LDRRM Fund but failed to submit to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the BAC Secretariat to submit the lacking documentary requirements to facilitate the auditorial, legal and technical evaluation of the contracts.

We recommended that the LCE require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to remit the balance of P611,191.00 for CY 2018 to avoid penalties for late remittance. Also, require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to conduct verification of the previous years’ records to establish the veracity of the balances of the account, and prepare/submit a remittance list as basis of the remittance to the BIR.
observations.reason: The CY 2018 balance of P611,191.00 and the previous balance of P1,136,016.80 remained unremitted as of December 31, 2019. Further, the Municipal Accountant—Designate is in the process of gathering the previous years’ records to establish the previous year’s balance of the account due to BIR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (a) make correcting entries to effect the necessary adjustments to arrive at the correct balances of the unliquidated cash advances; (b) prepare/maintain subsidiary ledgers for the balances of the advances accounts; and henceforth, exercise diligence in the posting of amounts to the general ledger. Also require the Municipal Accountant be required to refrain from granting cash advances unless the previous ones are still fully liquidated and accounted for in the books. Likewise, prepare adjusting entries to record the cash advances for payment of wages totaling P12,199,587.27 which were erroneously recorded in the Advances to Special Disbursing Officer.
observations.reason: The previous Municipal Treasurer still failed to turnover the undeposited collections and the process to gather previous years records and effect correcting entries is ongoing; some cash advances were still not fully liquidated and subsidiary ledgers were still not maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from allocating and to be utilizing portion of the 20% DF to non-programs/projects/activities and require the Municipal Accountant and BAC Secretariat to submit the lacking documents for audit.
observations.reason: Some lacking documents were still not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from using the SEF for expenditures not in conformity with the relevant law and regulation.

We recommended that Management see to it that disbursements of items of expenditures should not exceed the approved appropriation. Likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer to submit for audit the liquidation of the outstanding cash advances for wages of JO personnel.
observations.reason: Some outstanding liquidations and prior cash advance liquidations were still pending; previous Municipal Treasurer failed to submit some liquidation reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant draw a journal entry voucher to record the donated equipment to its proper asset account and provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements. Moreover require the Property and Supply unit to record the other in-kind donations in the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM and prepare monthly reports on the receipt and utilization of the same. Henceforth, adhere strictly to COA Circulars 2012-002 and 2014-002 on the accounting and reporting of the LDRRMF.
We recommended that management strictly comply with the provisions set forth under RA 9184.

We recommended that the management strictly follow the menu plan set by the DSWD and to furnish the audit team of the monthly physical accomplishment and financial report on the utilization of funds and quarterly report on nutritional status of children.
observations.reason: Monthly physical accomplishment were implemented but were not submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit the lacking 27 paid disbursement vouchers amounting to P11,499,747.01 to COA for audit and verification.
observations.reason: No additional submission of the aforementioned disbursement vouchers in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Health Officer ascertain that requisitions for drugs and medicines conform with the Philippine National Drug Formulary (PNDF) Vol. 1 prior to procurement, and require from supplier the Certificate of Product Registration (CPR) from Bureau of Food and Drugs, pursuant to Joint DOH-DTI-IPO-BFAD Administrative Order No. 2008-01 dated November 8, 2008. Likewise, record the medical apparatuses worth P109,074.00 as Medical Equipment Account in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.
observations.reason: Some requisitions of medicines were not supported with CPR from BFAD. Some medicines procurement procedures were improved; some medical apparatuses were recorded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant render their accounts and submit the financial reports in accordance with Section 105 of PD 1445 and COA Circulars 2009-006 and 2010-001.
observations.reason: Submission of some disbursement vouchers and reports were delayed due to heavy workload, lack of manpower and time constraints; some reconciliations and inventories were still pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that procurement for goods and services for implementation of various infrastructure projects be made through public bidding or other alternative modes of procurement in accordance with the guidelines set forth in the Revised RA 9184 and its implementing rules and regulations. Also, management should refrain from paying claims not supported by basic supporting documents required in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.reason: Some procurement procedures were improved; reimbursements on a few items were still made on reimbursement basis although improvements were ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers/Records for the controlling account balances appearing in the General Ledger and provide details and the details of the non-moving/dormant accounts.
observations.managementAction: Issued memorandum to the Municipal Accountant on the timely preparation of the bank reconciliation statements. (Note: duplicated action in table).

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all accountable officers be required to settle immediately their outstanding cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should see to it that liquidation documents are complete in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented. (No action taken).

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and the Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer be required to submit the lacking supporting documents on the payment of Social Pension to beneficiaries amounting to P1,429,500.00 and on the purchase of food commodities for Supplemental Feeding Program in the total amount of P570,154.00. Henceforth, adhere strictly to Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and RA 9184 and its IRR.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management disburse funds only when supported with complete documentation as required under Section 4(6) of PD 1445. Likewise, require the SWO to formulate guidelines on the financial assistance to indigents and submit the same to sanggunian for approval pursuant to Section 483(b) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the SWO to formulate policies/guidelines on the financial assistance. The SWO did not submit the guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit the financial reports in accordance with Section 105 of PD 1445 and COA Circular Nos. 2009-006 and 2010-001 and render their accounts, and submit the lacking supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Several disbursement vouchers, including cancelled checks, were submitted to the audit team in a staggered basis but inventory is yet to be made. No action was made on the 53 issued checks which were unrecorded in the books.
observations.reason: On the other hand, submission of reports was delayed due to heavy workload, lack of manpower and time constraint.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the provisions of Section 69 of PD 1445 and Section 32 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Collections totaling P336,481.51 of the previous Municipal Treasurer remained undeposited as of 12/31/2018.

observations.recommendation:
1.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive demand the previous
Municipal Treasurer to turnover immediately the undeposited collections
amounting to P336,481.51 to the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and the latter to
deposit the same to the LGU depository bank.

2.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant-Designate to a) submit the bank statements for all bank accounts
for the period September to December 2018; b) prepare a JEV for the
cancellation of the stale checks; and c) see to it that the balance per books in
the BRS is the balance appearing in the general ledger. Likewise, require the
timely preparation of BRS for all bank accounts and to submit the same
together with the bank statements to the Audit Team for review and
verification.

3.13 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the specific public
infrastructures acquired prior to CY 2015 to the appropriate Infrastructure
Assets accounts and maintain property ledger cards for each of the public
infrastructures. Likewise, prepare a lapsing schedule to compute
depreciation of each depreciable PPE.

4.6 We recommended that Management strictly observe the guidelines on the
appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund and see to it
that the fund is optimally utilized during the year to help achieve the
desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the
LGU.

5.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
1. the Budget Officer to strictly appropriate the LDRRM fund for
disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response,
rehabilitation and recovery. Also, to maintain separate registries of
appropriation, allotment and obligations for the unexpended
LDRRMF of the previous years.
2. the MDRRM Officer to prepare an annual LDRRMF Investment
Plan for the current and the unexpended LDRRMF of the previous
years.
3. the Accountant to submit the disbursement vouchers and
supporting documents totaling P2,723,007.00.

6.6 We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) include only
PPA’s that will address emerging and/or continuing issues and concerns of
the constituents and employees such as the provision of basic services and
facilities to protect women’s rights, economic empowerment and
participation in local governance, and monitor the implementation of the
GPB.

8.6 We recommended that the LCE require the BAC Secretariat to submit the
lacking documentary requirements to facilitate the auditorial, legal and
technical evaluation of the contracts.

9.5 We recommended that the LCE require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to
remit the balance of P611,191.00 for CY 2018 to avoid penalties for late
remittance. Also, require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to conduct

observations.managementAction: AAR No. 9 For CY 2017 the appropriation amounted to already 5% of the Annual Budget while the GAD Plan and the Report on Utilization of the Fund were submitted for audit.
observations.reason: Implemented; GAD Plan and utilization report submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant- Designate be directed to prepare and submit (a) Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts for the year ended December 31, 2015; and (b) explanatory notes on financial instruments and other disclosures to include reconciliation between actual amounts on a comparable basis as presented in the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts and in the Statement of Financial Performance for the year ended December 31, 2015, as well as reconciliation of net cash flows from operating activities to surplus (deficit). Likewise require the Municipal Budget Officer-Designate to submit the Statement of Appropriations, Allotments, Obligations and Balances (SAAOB) for CY 2015 in accordance with COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Financial statements for CY 2015 were submitted on February 20, 2017 or 6 days delayed, and the same was still incomplete because the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts was lacking as of reporting date. (See Findings No. 1, Part II of this report)
observations.reason: Disregard of pertinent rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly comply with the provisions set forth under RA 9184 and its IRR and to submit all lacking documents as documentary which will be required in the Notices of Suspension that will be subsequently issued.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Findings: Observers were not invited at some stages of procurement and the submitted contracts and payments therefor were unsupported with complete documentation. (See Finding No. 5, Part II of this report)
observations.reason: Disregard of pertinent rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant submit a copy of the LDRRMP and require the Municipal Accountant to prepare, maintain and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF with subsidiary ledgers and separate registries in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Findings: The LDRRMF for CY 2015 remain unsubmitted as of reporting date; and subsidiary ledgers and separate registries were not prepared and submitted to COA for evaluation.
observations.reason: Disregard of the prescribed law, rules and regulations, including the failure to prepare subsidiary ledgers and separate registries therefor.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit the list and details of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented during the year pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Findings: Said list remained unsubmitted to COA for evaluation.
observations.reason: Disregard of the prescribed regulation and audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for the balances of the General Ledger and provide for the details of the non-moving accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Findings: Still, no subsidiary ledgers were prepared and maintained for the balances in the General Ledger and details of non-moving accounts were not yet determined.
observations.reason: Due to voluminous workload of the Municipal Accountant and lack of manpower knowledgeable for the task in the accounting office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly implement the provision of Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; transfer unexpended balances to a separate DRRM Trust Fund; and prepare the LDRRM Plan and Report of Sources and Application of Funds and submit the same to COA for evaluation.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Findings: Prior years’ unexpended balances of the LDRRMF as of 12/31/2016 and of previous years were not yet transferred to a Separate DRRM Trust Fund; LDRRM Plan for CY 2016 was not submitted; The Report of Sources and Application of the Funds for CY 2016 and of prior years remain unsubmitted.
observations.reason: Without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension as a result of the post-audit of transactions; non-compliance with DILG and COA circular requirements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer be required to prepare
and submit to the Municipal Accountant the Statement of Appropriations,
Allotments, Obligations and Balances (SAAOB) to facilitate the preparation of
the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts for the year ended
December 31, 2016.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to
recognize depreciation on property, plant and equipment for CY 2016 and 2015
in accordance with PPSAS No. 17. Likewise, direct the inventory committee to
conduct physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment and render a report
thereon, in accordance with Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and the
Handbook on Property and Supply Management.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant draw a journal entry
voucher to record the donated equipment to its proper asset account and provide
adequate disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements. Moreover, require the
Property and Supply unit to record the other in-kind donations in the Registry
of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM and prepare monthly reports on the receipt
35
and utilization of the same. Henceforth, adhere strictly to COA Circulars 2012-
002 and 2014-002 on the accounting and reporting of the LDRRMF.

We recommend that the BAC be required to invite observers in all stages
of the procurement process and strictly comply with the posting of the Notice of
Award and Notice to Proceed, with the approved contract, in the PhilGEPS
website and conspicuous place of the entity. Likewise, submit the lacking
documents relative to the procurements made during the year.

We recommend that the menu plan set by the DSWD be strictly followed
in order to meet the nutritional needs of children for their development and
growth. Likewise, furnish the audit team of the monthly physical
accomplishment and financial report on the utilization of funds and quarterly
report on nutritional status of children beneficiaries as provided for in the
MOA.

We recommend for the termination of the services of the Solid Waste
Management Consultant and Coordinator and instead, advance the formation of
the Solid Waste Management Board and the formulation of the Solid Waste
Management Plan pursuant to Sections 10 and 12 of Republic Act No. 9003.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to submit the
corresponding disbursement voucher and supporting documents of cash
advance for CIF within seven (7) days after release of the check, as required
under the Joint Circular.

We recommend that management submit the lacking 27 paid
disbursement vouchers amounting to P11,499,747.01 to COA for audit and
verification.

We recommend for the strict application of the provisions on
appointment of municipal officials and employees set forth under RA 7160 and
other civil service rules and regulations and to abstain from paying travel
allowance to personnel of job order status, as it is allowed only to government
employees.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant: (a) make correcting entries to effect the adjustments in arriving at the correct balances of the cash advances; (b) prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for cash advances; and (c) henceforth, exercise diligence in the posting of amounts to the general ledger.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Findings: a. No correcting entry was made to effect the adjustments in arriving at the correct balance of the cash advances; b. The newly appointed Municipal Accountant has prepared subsidiary ledgers for cash advances but the accounts reflected outstanding cash advances start from July 2016 only, or from the time she assumed office. However, she made sure to start work on it.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant asserted that she cannot come up with the complete subsidiary ledgers right away due to time constraint and workload. However, she made sure to start the work on it.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to recognize depreciation on property, plant and equipment for CY 2015 in accordance with PPSAS No. 17. Likewise, direct the inventory committee to conduct physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment and render a report thereon, in accordance with Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and the Handbook on Property and Supply Management.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Findings: a. Management still failed to provide for depreciation for CY 2015 for the said PPE accounts. This has been reiterated in current year’s finding as CY 2016 PPE accounts were likewise not provided with depreciation; and b. Inventory was still not conducted on all properties of the Municipal agency.
observations.reason: Disregard of pertinent rules and audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all accountable officers be required to settle immediately their outstanding cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should see to it that liquidations are complete in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Findings: The current management advances. Accountable officers and employees were likewise required to liquidate their outstanding cash advances and reconcile. But despite the same, there are still prior years’ cash advances which cannot be identified and reconciled.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has placed controls in the grant and liquidation of cash advances. Accountable officers and employees were required to liquidate. However, there are still prior years’ advances which cannot be reconciled due to previous years’ lack of internal control on cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Health Officer of the LGU ascertain that requisitions for drugs and medicines conform with the PNDF while the supplier should be strictly required to submit the Certificate of Product Registration (CPR) from Bureau of Food and Drugs, pursuant to Joint DOH-DTI-IPO-BFAD Administrative Order No. 2008-01 dated November 8, 2008. Likewise, record the medical apparatuses worth P109,074.00 as Medical Equipment Account in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Findings: a. Medicines procured during the year conform with the PNDF while the requisitions for drugs and medicines remain not submitted; b. No correcting entry was made to book up the medical equipment worth P109,074.00.
observations.reason: Disregard of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer and the Municipal Accountant be required to submit the lacking supporting documents on the payment of Social Pension and accounting of food commodities. Likewise, implementation of the Supplemental Feeding Program should be sufficiently documented and henceforth adhere strictly to Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and RA 9184 and its IRR.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Findings: The lacking supporting documents on the payment of Social Pension remain unsubmitted; Implementation of the Supplemental Feeding Program of the municipality remain insufficiently documented (See Finding No. 2, Part II of this report).
observations.reason: Foregoing transactions were supported but some documents remain unsubmitted; correspondingly the purchase of food commodities lacked complete supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee strictly adhere with RA 9184 and its IRR in resorting to alternative method of procurement and ensure transparency of procurement thru proper posting and invitation to observers. Likewise, see to it that payment of spare parts and gasoline are supported with complete documents in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and Sections 361 and 362 of COA Circular No. 91-368 dated December 19, 1991.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Findings: Substantial compliance with the law, rules and regulations is being observed by the current administration. The management has placed controls in the grant and liquidation of cash advances. Accountable officers and employees were likewise required to liquidate their outstanding cash advances. But despite the same, there are still prior years’ cash advances which cannot be identified and reconciled.
observations.reason: Management is currently working for full compliance with the regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management stop paying claims for meal expenses during travels of local officials except to what is authorized as per diems pursuant to Executive Order No. 298, and document properly all valid claims for representation expenses in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Findings: Payment of meals during official travels, aside from that already included in per diems, was prohibited.
observations.reason: Payment of meals during official travels, aside from that already included in per diems, was prohibited.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that payments of financial and burial assistance be disbursed only when supported with complete required documentation as required under Section 4(6) of PD 1445. Likewise, the MSWDO should formulate policies/guidelines on the grant of financial assistance and submit the same to Sanggunian for approval pursuant to Section 483(b) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Findings: Payment of financial and burial assistance are already sufficiently supported with required documents as required under Section 4(6) of PD 1445. Likewise, the MSWDO still failed to formulate the policies and guidelines on the grant of financial assistance and submit the same to the sanggunian for approval pursuant to Section 483(b) of RA 7160.
observations.reason: Several payments were supported with documentary requirements; however, the MSWDO still failed to formulate and submit the policies and guidelines to the Sanggunian.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local School Board prepare the Annual Board Budget for SEF to fund priority projects/programs of public elementary and secondary schools in the locality. Moreover, submit the disbursement voucher on cash advance amounting to P50,000.00 including the lacking supporting documents to the liquidation report, and henceforth adhere strictly to Section 4(6) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Findings: The Local School Board still failed to prepare the Annual Budget for CY 2016; and the disbursement voucher on cash advance amounting to P50,000.00 remain unsubmitted including the lacking supporting documents to the liquidation report.
observations.reason: Disbursement voucher and some supporting documents were submitted but still lacking; compliance partly affected by oversight and documentation issues.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant-Designate render their accounts, and submit the financial reports in accordance with Section 105 of PD 1445 and COA Circular Nos. 2009-006 and 2010-001.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Findings: Several disbursement vouchers, including cancelled checks, were submitted as compliance, but some of the transactions were still to be verified. Submission of some reports was not on time due to heavy workload and lack of manpower knowledgeable for the task in the accounting office.
observations.reason: Other reports and supporting documents were late due to manpower constraint and heavy workload of the Municipal Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal management submit the annual financial statements on or before February 14 of each year and require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare the SAAOB and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant on due time for inclusion in the reports to be submitted to the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Findings: The submission of the financial statements and schedules of the municipality, including other accounts and financial reports, was still delayed while the Municipal Budget Officer still failed to prepare the SAAOB and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for audit. The submission of the annual financial reports and schedules of the municipality was still delayed while the Municipal Budget Officer still failed to prepare the SAAOB and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Non-observance of the reglementary period on the submission of the financial accounts and reports; failure of the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare SAAOB.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require all accountable officers concerned to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances and direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce Section 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 that no additional cash advance shall be allowed to any internal official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled and that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for all cash advances to support the balance of cash advances reflected in the general ledger.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Findings: Still, a huge amount of 2014 cash advances remain unliquidated at year-end. However, the current management has placed controls in the grant and liquidation of cash advances. Accountable officers and employees were likewise required to liquidate their outstanding cash advances and should reconcile. But despite the same, there are still previous years’ cash advances which cannot be identified and reconciled.
observations.reason: Several prior years’ outstanding cash advances remain unliquidated; difficulty in identification due to lack of supporting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: (a) incorporate in the succeeding AIP of the municipality the continuing appropriation of 20% DF; (b) refrain from allocating retained portion of 20% DF to MOOE or as subsidies to barangays; and (c) submit report on the utilization of the fund.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Findings: Prior year’s balance of 20% EDF remained unpresented. Subsidies to barangays were retained but in the form of projects already. Utilization report of the 20% DF was not submitted to COA for evaluation.
observations.reason: Disregard of regulation; though some 20% EDF projects were implemented, the utilization report remained unsubmitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: (a) require the concerned job order personnel to submit DTRs and accomplishment reports; and (b) indicate in the job order contract the duties and responsibilities of the JO personnel and their actual immediate supervisors; and for LGU-paid teachers, submission of the certification from the school’s principal signifying rendition of services of the respective municipal-paid teachers.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Findings: A number of prior years’ cash advances still remained unliquidated at year-end. However, the current management has placed controls in the grant and liquidation of cash advances. Accountable officers and employees were likewise required to liquidate their outstanding cash advances and should reconcile. But despite the same, there are still previous years’ cash advances which cannot be identified and reconciled.
observations.reason: Failure to comply with duties and documentation by job order personnel; lack of printed names and certifications; duties not indicated in job order contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer strictly observe the provisions of Section 69 of PD 1445 and Section 32 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Findings: A number of prior years’ undeposited collections remain at year-end. Deposits are not done regularly. The Treasurer’s Office remain unstrict in the implementation of the regulations.
observations.reason: Non-observance of regulations and implementational laxity by the Treasurer’s Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct all accountable officers concerned to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances and direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for all cash advances to support the balance of cash advances reflected in the general ledger.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Findings: A number of prior years’ cash advances still remained outstanding at year-end. However, the current management has already placed controls in the grant and liquidation of cash advances. Accountable officers and employees were likewise required to liquidate their outstanding cash advances and reconcile. But despite the same, there are still previous years’ cash advances which cannot be identified and reconciled.
observations.reason: Several prior years’ cash advances still remained outstanding and could not be reconciled due to lack of subsidiary ledgers and records.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant strictly implement the provisions of Sections 114 and 119 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume I and Section 375 of RA 7160. We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to take up the purchase of heavy equipment in the books.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Findings: The current management has started complying with the preparation and maintenance of property, plant and equipment ledger cards; however, inventory of all the properties of the agency was still not conducted; and heavy equipment amounting to P3,492,000.00 was still unrecorded in the books.
observations.reason: Although management started compliance, the inventory and full recording of heavy equipment remain pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly implement the provision of DILG-DBM Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 as to the funding source of the 20% Economic Development Fund and that all proposed programs/projects should be implemented to achieve desirable socio-economic development.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Findings: All proposed projects under CY 2016 20% EDF partake in the nature of capital expenditures and have been implemented.
observations.reason: Although some proposed projects were implemented, management was reminded to adhere to the guidelines under the DILG-DBM Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 as to the appropriation and utilization of the fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant require the rendering of accounts and submit the lacking disbursement vouchers and supporting documents and to record all transactions made by the LGU in the books in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160 and COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 to come up with a reliable financial statements presentation for the year under audit.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 Findings: Several transactions were submitted but some of the unrecorded issued checks remain unlocated. Compliance was done in a staggered basis but the 8 issued checks were still to be recorded.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents were submitted but some issued checks remain unlocated and unrecorded.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management ensure procurement of supplies, equipment and materials be made thru public bidding or other alternative modes of procurement in accordance with the guidelines set forth under RA 9184 and its IRR. Also, refrain from paying claims which are not supported by basic supporting documents as required in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 Findings: Several transactions relative to procurement during the year were made directly to suppliers thru shopping or negotiated procurement. Substantial documentation was made on the transactions however some supporting documents were still lacking.
observations.reason: Some documentary requirements were made but several transactions still lacked basic supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to render their accounts and submit the supporting documents and disbursement vouchers immediately together with the supporting documents for audit as required by COA Circulars.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011 Findings: Several disbursement vouchers and supporting documents were submitted to the audit team in a staggered basis but the 53 issued checks were still to be recorded. No action was done on the 53 issued checks which were unrecorded in the books.
observations.reason: Some vouchers and documents submitted but 53 issued checks remain unrecorded and outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly observe the provisions of the Annual General Appropriations Act and DBM-NEDA-CW/PCW Joint Circular No. 2004-001 dated April 5, 2004 and prepare the GAD Plan and Accomplishment Report and the implementation of the GAD Plan be addressed.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 Findings: Appropriation for the 2012 year amounted to P300,000.00 and the Annual Budget. The Annual GAD Plan was prepared and the Annual Budget already reflected the 5% but the Accomplishment Report for correspondingly was not submitted for audit.
observations.reason: Although the Annual GAD Plan was prepared by the LGU and many projects implemented, the Accomplishment Report was not submitted for audit.
